rsts 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

rsts

rsts provides an interface to performing Slay the Spire run analysis using R.

Installation

You can install the development version of rsts like so:

pak::pkg_install("fwimp/rsts")

Basic Usage

To load your own run data, you can run the load_sts_history() function.

library(rsts)
myruns <- load_sts_history()

If you are on windows, this should automatically load your runs, however if you are on another platform or are having troubles you can specify the path to load manually.

Note: if you provide an explicit path, you should also set the id= argument to be your steam community ID (i.e. a 17-character number, usually starting with 7). This will help with working out who is “you” on multiplayer runs.

library(rsts)
myruns <- load_sts_history("path/to/runhistory", id=76561198000000000)
length(myruns)
#> [1] 18

Filters can be applied using the filter_ methods:

justwins <- myruns$filter_outcome("Win")
length(justwins)
#> [1] 4

They can also be chained:

ironcladwins <- myruns$filter_outcome("Win")$filter_character("Ironclad", onlyowner = TRUE)
length(ironcladwins)
#> [1] 1

AI Statement

Absolutely no AI or LLM tools were used in the development of this package. This was initially a toy problem upon which to learn R6 classes in R, which spiralled out of control rather quickly.
